heading: Failure to pay legal child support obligations
label: 228 Failure to pay legal child support obligations

children.text: willfully fails to pay a support obligation with respect to a child who resides in another State, if such obligation has remained unpaid for a period longer than 1 year, or is greater than $5,000;
travels in interstate or foreign commerce with the intent to evade a support obligation, if such obligation has remained unpaid for a period longer than 1 year, or is greater than $5,000; or
willfully fails to pay a support obligation with respect to a child who resides in another State, if such obligation has remained unpaid for a period longer than 2 years, or is greater than $10,000;

children.heading: .—Presumption
children.label: b .—Presumption
children.text: The existence of a support obligation that was in effect for the time period charged in the indictment or information creates a rebuttable presumption that the obligor has the ability to pay the support obligation for that time period.
in the case of a first offense under subsection (a)(1), a fine under this title, imprisonment for not more than 6 months, or both; and

in the case of an offense under paragraph (2) or (3) of subsection (a), or a second or subsequent offense under subsection (a)(1), a fine under this title, imprisonment for not more than 2 years, or both.

children.heading: .—Mandatory Restitution
children.label: d .—Mandatory Restitution
children.text: Upon a conviction under this section, the court shall order restitution under section 3663A in an amount equal to the total unpaid support obligation as it exists at the time of sentencing.

the district in which the child who is the subject of the support obligation involved resided during a period during which a person described in subsection (a) (referred to in this subsection as an “obliger”) failed to meet that support obligation;
the district in which the obliger resided during a period described in paragraph (1); or
any other district with jurisdiction otherwise provided for by law.
the term “Indian tribe” has the meaning given that term in section 102 of the Federally Recognized Indian Tribe List Act of 1994 ();25 U.S.C. 479a1See References in Text note below.1
the term “State” includes any State of the United States, the District of Columbia, and any commonwealth, territory, or possession of the United States; and

the term “support obligation” means any amount determined under a court order or an order of an administrative process pursuant to the law of a State or of an Indian tribe to be due from a person for the support and maintenance of a child or of a child and the parent with whom the child is living.
